---
title: "Livestock Marketing Alternatives Explorer"
subtitle: "An open, continuously-updated explorer of public-domain weekly feeder-cattle price data for the four-corners region."
toc: false
---

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| label: read-anchors

# Read the latest Clovis auction date and CPI vintage at render time so any
# inline reference to "this week" or "latest" on this page can be resolved
# to a concrete date the reader can verify against. If a manifest is
# missing (e.g., a fresh clone before the first pipeline run), we fall
# back to a string so the page still renders.

import json
from pathlib import Path

PROCESSED = Path("..") / "data" / "processed"


def _latest_vintage(manifest_filename: str) -> str:
    path = PROCESSED / manifest_filename
    if not path.exists():
        return "(not yet populated)"
    try:
        with path.open("r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
            entries = json.load(f).get("entries", [])
        if entries:
            v = entries[-1].get("vintage")
            if v:
                return str(v)
    except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):
        pass
    return "(unavailable)"


latest_clovis_auction = _latest_vintage("clovis_MANIFEST.json")
latest_cpi_vintage = _latest_vintage("MANIFEST.json")
```

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
**Current status**: active prototype. Live chart pages cover price–weight, weekly seasonality, weekly trends, basis to CME GF feeder futures, sell-now compare (Clovis, NM feeder-cattle auction), and an LRP explorer (USDA RMA Summary of Business). Live data feeds: USDA-AMS Clovis (sale-week cadence), BLS CPI-U (monthly), USDA RMA (annual). Latest Clovis auction on file: **`{python} latest_clovis_auction`**.
:::

## What this is

A producer-facing, Extension-aligned platform that takes weekly USDA-AMS feeder-cattle market data for the four-corners region and turns it into focused, interactive figures — price-weight relationships, weekly seasonality, basis to the nearby CME feeder-futures contract — refreshed automatically as new public USDA and BLS data land. The cash-side numbers (USDA-AMS, BLS) are sourced from public-domain federal datasets; the basis charts are a derived statistic computed against CME GF settles which are not themselves redistributed (see [data licensing](https://github.com/lma-explorer/lma-explorer.github.io/blob/main/LICENSE-DATA.md)). Every figure is reproducible from versioned code; every page has an **edit on GitHub** link for corrections.

## Who this is for

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
### For producers

A handful of focused, mobile-friendly pages that answer the questions rural producers actually ask: *What did cattle bring last week at weight X?* *Is it better to sell now or retain?* *What does a slide contract cost me in a given market?*

[Start with weekly trends →](weekly-trends.qmd)
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
### For Extension peers & graduate students

Each figure has a **methodology** link explaining what's computed, the deflation choice, and the data vintage. All code is MIT-licensed; every page has an "Edit on GitHub" link so a correction or a state-level adaptation is a pull request away.

[Read the methodology →](methodology/index.qmd)
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
### For researchers & journalists

Raw snapshots are committed as Parquet, with annual Zenodo DOI releases planned (a roadmap item — the first release will land once the platform stabilizes). Every pipeline run is logged in the `MANIFEST.json`; every figure carries a vintage stamp.

[Browse the data →](data.qmd)
:::

:::

## What should I look at first?

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
**"I want to know what cattle brought last week."** → [Weekly trends](weekly-trends.qmd) — actual auction-week prices on a calendar timeline.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
**"I want to compare weight classes."** → [Price–weight](price-weight.qmd) — min/mean/max bands across your selected window.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
**"I want to know if this week is high or low."** → [Sell-now compare](sell-now-compare.qmd) — this week vs. the historical band for the same week-of-year.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
**"I hedge or watch futures."** → [Basis](basis.qmd) — Clovis cash minus CME GF settle, by week-of-year.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
**"I want to know when prices peak each year."** → [Weekly seasonality](seasonality.qmd) — week-of-year pattern, all weight classes overlaid.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
**"I want a printable reference for the sale or banker."** → [Producer reference card (PDF)](one-pager.pdf) — one printable sheet with the latest auction's prices.
:::

:::

## Start here: is this week good or bad?

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple" icon="false"}
**[→ Sell-now compare: is this week's price high or low for this time of year?](sell-now-compare.qmd)**

For each weight class × bin, the page shows where this week's actual Clovis price sits relative to the historical band for the same calendar week of past years. A green dot means top 25% of past years; gray middle 50%; red bottom 25%. One input to a marketing decision, not a recommendation.

*"This week" anchored on Clovis auction date: **`{python} latest_clovis_auction`**.* If a regular weekly auction was missed (holiday, USDA reporting delay, ingest failure), this is the most recent published auction — not necessarily the most recent calendar week.
:::

## Four views of the same dataset

The chart shelf decomposes weekly Clovis prices and CME-feeder basis along four orthogonal axes — pick the page that matches the question you're asking. Each is exploratory; the sell-now compare above is decision-anchored.

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-3}
### [Price–weight explorer](price-weight.qmd)

*"What does each weight class fetch on average?"* Min/mean/max bands across the selected window, one panel for Steers and one for Heifers.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-3}
### [Weekly seasonality](seasonality.qmd)

*"When in the year do these prices peak?"* All six weight classes overlaid on a 1–52 week-of-year axis; intra-year pattern net of trend.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-3}
### [Weekly trends](weekly-trends.qmd)

*"Where are we now vs. recent history?"* Actual auction-week observations on a calendar timeline, October 2017 to present.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-3}
### [Basis & weight-class spreads](basis.qmd)

*"What's my hedge basis to the May / November / Nearby feeder contract — and what about non-target weight classes?"* Strict basis for 700–799 lb Steers; cash–futures spreads for the other weight bins and Heifers as cycle context.
:::

:::

All four pages share the same year-window selector (All / Last 5 yrs / Last 3 yrs / latest year) and the same nominal-vs-real dollar toggle. The basis page adds a contract-month selector (May / November / Nearby) on top of those two; real-dollar basis is computed per row as `basis_nominal × deflator(month_of_auction)` because both cash and futures settle scale by the same monthly CPI factor.

## A different kind of view: livestock insurance

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple" icon="false"}
**[→ Livestock Risk Protection (LRP): what does the program look like?](lrp.qmd)**

The chart shelf above describes weekly Clovis cash auction data and its relationship to CME GF feeder futures. The LRP page describes a different kind of producer choice — federal price-floor insurance for feeder cattle (USDA RMA's [Livestock Risk Protection program](https://www.rma.usda.gov/policy-and-procedure/insurance-plans/livestock-risk-protection-policy)). The State dropdown defaults to Arizona; the Year window button row toggles between All available years, Last 5 yrs, Last 3 yrs, and Latest. Four groups of information are surfaced: participation, cost/subsidy, coverage design, and outcome/payout. The page is descriptive — it shows what the program looks like, not whether to buy it.
:::

## Producer reference card (PDF)

::: {.callout-tip appearance="simple"}
**For producers heading to the sale or a banker meeting**: download the [**This Week at Clovis** producer reference card (PDF)](one-pager.pdf). One printable sheet with the latest auction's M&L 1 prices by weight bin × class, a 26-week mini-trend per class, and a vintage stamp. Refreshed every time the Clovis pipeline commits a new snapshot.
:::

## Last refreshed

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| label: last-refreshed

# Surface the two highest-cadence data sources' vintages on the home page so
# readers don't have to navigate to the data catalog to see freshness.
# Anchors set in the read-anchors chunk above; this re-reads the manifest's
# "written_at_utc" for the timestamp display.

def _written_at(manifest_filename: str) -> str:
    path = PROCESSED / manifest_filename
    if not path.exists():
        return "(not yet populated)"
    try:
        with path.open("r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
            entries = json.load(f).get("entries", [])
        if entries:
            return entries[-1].get("written_at_utc", "(unavailable)") or "(unavailable)"
    except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):
        pass
    return "(unavailable)"


print(f"- **Clovis auction (sale-week)**: latest auction `{latest_clovis_auction}` · pipeline last wrote `{_written_at('clovis_MANIFEST.json')}`")
print(f"- **BLS CPI-U (monthly)**: latest period `{latest_cpi_vintage}` · pipeline last wrote `{_written_at('MANIFEST.json')}`")
```

For the full inventory of every snapshot the platform commits — including basis, Era B historical reconstruction, and LRP — see the [data catalog](data.qmd).

## Governance

This platform is maintained in the open. The guiding commitment is [**Automation Over Features**](https://github.com/lma-explorer/lma-explorer.github.io/blob/main/GOVERNANCE.md): every new page must be scheduled, must fail loudly, must fall back to last-known-good on failure, must carry a vintage stamp, must have a methodology page, and must be license-compliant — before it ships.

Content: [CC-BY-4.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/). Code: [MIT](https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT). Citation: [CITATION.cff](https://github.com/lma-explorer/lma-explorer.github.io/blob/main/CITATION.cff).
